blurb: Apple Pay replaces payment card numbers with a Device Account Number (DAN) provisioned directly to the Secure Element – a certified EMVCo/PCI-DSS hardware security module. The real card PAN is never stored on device or transmitted to merchants. Each transaction generates a unique payment cryptogram using the DAN plus a transaction-specific nonce, making each authorization one-time-use and replay-resistant. The NFC controller exclusively routes payment data to and from the Secure Element, never exposing it to the application processor or iOS. Face ID or Touch ID gates every transaction, providing biometric confirmation of user intent before the Secure Element executes the payment.
